Uploaded patch set 4.

Patch Set 4:

James, any trouble forseen on this one?  BUILD_VERSION is already pretty generic, so the new name isn't much worse...

Right now it looks like the upstream kernel code is setting the version string in two different hardcoded places, and this gets rid of that.

Of course upstream won't have PACKAGE_VERSION set, but you could hard code that in lustre_ver.h instead, just like you have to do with LUSTRE_VERSION_STRING, and the other stuff.  Actually, there probably isn't much reason not to jump right to using LUSTRE_VERSION_STRING where we currently use BUILD_VERSION.  I had a reason originally for not doing that all in one step though...can't remember what it is right now.

parents.subject: LU-6601 osp: update obd status while osp status changed

commit.subject: LU-7699 build: Eliminate lustre_build_version.h
commit.message:
LU-7699 build: Eliminate lustre_build_version.h

The lustre_build_version.h is no longer necessary.  With the new
versioning system the lustre version can now be retrieved from
standard autotools defines and/or lustre_ver.h.

To make compatibility easier for the upstream lustre client in
the linux kernel, we use LUSTRE_VERSION_STRING from lustre_ver.h.

Change-Id: I08233d8547bc4af7bf2fb0c15bee8e306f6fa0f0
Signed-off-by: Christopher J. Morrone <morrone2@llnl.gov>
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/18108
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Reviewed-by: Dmitry Eremin <dmitry.eremin@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
